seed: Quantum mechanics can be extended from a single space-time Hilbert space H_xt to an enlarged dual-manifold Hilbert space H_total = H_xt ⊕ H_kE, where the momentum-energy sector H_kE has its own autonomous unitary evolution in energy generated by a self-adjoint time operator T. This dual-manifold structure should imply two concrete consequences: a stationary random-phase background in H_kE Fourier-projects into H_xt as a finite homogeneous dark-energy-like density, and boundary consistency near asymptotic k-space forces an exponential map k(x)=k0 exp(-kappa x) equivalent to the horizon relation underlying Hawking radiation, without assuming general relativity.
